description:
This conceptual map illustrates a "Simple Change" in Land Cover / Land Use (LCLU) classification between two interpretations, "Interpretation 2012" and "Interpretation 2018".
The 2012 interpretation shows three distinct LCLU polygons:
1.  An outer, larger beige area classified as "2.1.1.0".
2.  A large red area, classified as "1.1.1.1", partially surrounded by the beige area.
3.  An inner, light pink polygon, classified as "1.4.0.0", entirely contained within the red "1.1.1.1" area.
The 2018 interpretation shows a change where the inner light pink polygon (previously "1.4.0.0") has transformed into the "1.1.1.1" class (red), effectively merging with the surrounding red area. The outer beige area (2.1.1.0) and its internal boundaries remain unchanged. This demonstrates a single polygon changing its LCLU class over time.
